title: Can not delete configmap key vaule related file using kube play
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue
podman kube play cannot delete configmap key vaule related file 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. prepare all in one k8s kube yaml file, then run `podman kube play \path\to\yaml`
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: busybox-cm
data:
  key1: value1
  key2: value2

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: busybox
  labels:
    name: busybox
spec:
  containers:
    - name: busybox
      image: "docker.io/library/busybox:latest"
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: "128Mi"
          cpu: "500m"
      args:
        - sleep
        - "3600"
      volumeMounts:
        - name: test
          mountPath: /mnt/test
  volumes:
    - name: test
      configMap:
        name: busybox-cm
  restartPolicy: Never
```

2. run `ls` command to get the files in dir `/mnt/test` which mount from configmap in container
```
$ podman exec busybox-busybox ls /mnt/test
key1
key2
```
3. update the kube yaml file, comment out the key2 in configmap, run again `podman kube play /path/to/file`
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: busybox-cm
data:
  key1: value1
  # key2: value2

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: busybox
  labels:
    name: busybox
spec:
  containers:
    - name: busybox
      image: "docker.io/library/busybox:latest"
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: "128Mi"
          cpu: "500m"
      args:
        - sleep
        - "3600"
      volumeMounts:
        - name: test
          mountPath: /mnt/test
  volumes:
    - name: test
      configMap:
        name: busybox-cm
  restartPolicy: Never
```
4. run `ls` command to get the files in dir `/mnt/test` which mount from configmap in container, the key2 still there
```
$ podman exec busybox-busybox ls /mnt/test
key1
key2
```


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

podman kube play cannot delete configmap key vaule related file 

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

podman kube play should update the configmap related volume when the data key in configmap yaml file was deleted 

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.

OS: FCOS 39

Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.8.2
API Version:  4.8.2
Go Version:   go1.21.4
Built:        Mon Dec 11 21:23:04 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

The issue is the volume has been created with the initial content.

```
$ podman volume ls
DRIVER      VOLUME NAME
local       busybox-cm

$ podman run -v busybox-cm:/tmp fedora ls /tmp
key1
key2
```

So the second time you run the play it sees that the volume exists and uses it.  If you remove the volume.

```
$ podman volume rm --force busybox-cm
WARN[0010] StopSignal SIGTERM failed to stop container busybox-busybox in 10 seconds, resorting to SIGKILL 
busybox-cm

$ podman kube play --replace /tmp/k.yaml
Pods stopped:
8652dc69fecd98e73da5851a4756d3278273114874437921c0ecdeb6ff202970
Pods removed:
8652dc69fecd98e73da5851a4756d3278273114874437921c0ecdeb6ff202970
Secrets removed:
Volumes removed:
Pod:
2ba35322e54f60576bf294f0b80200791f50a3d3b999db2ee034212ce57f9a76
Container:
85ef4b0e9dbfe58b4c4a73102b32b4546ad1eb1d7457de017f0222ebe94cbecb

$ podman exec busybox-busybox ls /mnt/test
key1
```

You could also do

$ podman kube down --force /tmp/k.yaml

Which is documented to remove the volumes.
